name: timeline
description: Canvas-based timeline/Gantt visualization with events, markers, sections, axes, zoom, and pan via TimelineCanvas React component and useTimeline hook.
when_to_use: Canvas-based timeline/Gantt visualization with events, markers, sections, axes, zoom, and pan via TimelineCanvas React component and useTimeline hook
avoid: Not for graph/flow diagrams — use @gravity-ui/graph. Not for bar/line/area charts — use chartkit or charts.
not_for: Not for graph/flow diagrams — use @gravity-ui/graph. Not for bar/line/area charts — use chartkit or charts.

name: AbstractEventRenderer
description: An abstract base class for custom timeline event renderers. Subclass it and implement render() to draw events on the canvas and getHitbox() to define the clickable area. The protected hitboxResult property provides a reusable Hitbox object to avoid allocations.
when_to_use: When the default event rendering is insufficient and a fully custom canvas drawing is needed
When events require a non-rectangular hitbox for click detection
avoid: Do not instantiate directly — it is abstract and must be subclassed

name: AbstractMarkerRenderer
description: An abstract base class for custom timeline marker renderers. Subclass it and implement render() to draw markers on the canvas. Receives full rendering context including hover/select state, position, view configuration, and a label size calculator.
when_to_use: When custom marker appearance is required beyond the default rendering
When markers need special label sizing or positional logic
avoid: Do not instantiate directly — it is abstract and must be subclassed

name: AbstractSectionRenderer
description: An abstract base class for custom timeline section renderers. Subclass it and implement render() to draw sections on the canvas and getHitbox() to define the interactive area. The protected hitboxResult property is reusable to reduce allocations.
when_to_use: When sections need custom canvas drawing beyond the built-in appearance
When section hit detection requires a custom bounding box
avoid: Do not instantiate directly — it is abstract and must be subclassed

name: TimelineCanvas
description: A React component that renders a Timeline instance onto an HTML canvas element. Calls timeline.init() on mount and timeline.destroy() on unmount. The canvas fills 100% of its parent container.
when_to_use: When rendering a Timeline instance in a React application
As the visual output component paired with useTimeline hook
For canvas-based timeline visualizations with events, markers, sections, and axes
avoid: Without a corresponding Timeline instance — always create one with useTimeline or new Timeline() first
When a DOM-based timeline is preferred over canvas rendering

props.name: timeline
props.description: Initialized Timeline instance to render on the canvas

props.name: className
props.description: CSS class applied to the canvas element

name: Timeline
description: The core Timeline class that manages canvas rendering, event/marker/section state, and user interaction (zoom, pan, click). Provides on/off/emit event emitter API and must be initialized with a canvas element via init().
when_to_use: When creating a timeline without React (plain JS/TS usage)
When fine-grained control over the timeline lifecycle is needed
avoid: In React applications — prefer useTimeline hook which memoizes the instance
signature: new Timeline<TEvent, TMarker, TSection>(config: TimeLineConfig): Timeline

name: useTimeline
description: Creates and memoizes a Timeline instance from a configuration object. Returns the timeline, its CanvasApi, and start/stop helpers for manual canvas initialization when not using TimelineCanvas.
when_to_use: To create a Timeline instance in a React component
When you need access to the CanvasApi for programmatic updates (setEvents, setMarkers, etc.)
As the primary factory hook before passing the timeline to TimelineCanvas
avoid: When only a static display is needed and no API calls are required — new Timeline() directly is also valid
signature: useTimeline<TEvent, TMarker, TSection>(config: TimeLineConfig<TEvent, TMarker, TSection>): { timeline: Timeline, api: CanvasApi, start: (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => void, stop: () => void }

parameters.name: config
parameters.description: Timeline configuration including settings (start, end, axes, events, markers, sections) and optional viewConfiguration

return_type: { timeline: Timeline, api: CanvasApi, start: (canvas) => void, stop: () => void }

name: useTimelineEvent
description: Subscribes to a specific Timeline API event (e.g. 'click', 'zoom', 'select') and automatically unsubscribes on cleanup. Wraps timeline.on/off in a useLayoutEffect so the listener is always in sync with the latest callback.
when_to_use: When reacting to user interactions on a timeline (clicks, zoom changes, selections)
As the idiomatic way to attach timeline event listeners in React without managing lifecycle manually
avoid: Do not call timeline.on() directly in React components — use this hook to avoid listener leaks on re-renders
signature: useTimelineEvent<Event extends keyof ApiEvent>(timeline: Timeline | null, event: Event, cb: (data: UnwrapTimelineEventsDetail, event: UnwrapTimelineEvents) => void): void

parameters.name: timeline
parameters.description: The Timeline instance to subscribe to; pass null to skip subscription

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The event name to listen to (e.g. 'click', 'zoom')

parameters.name: cb
parameters.description: Callback invoked with event detail and the raw CustomEvent
